description:
You are acting as an on-site penetration tester who has been asked to do a **very quick package-version reconnaissance** on the host you have just gained shell access to.  
Your customer only wants proof that you can pull the locally installed version of a single critical package (OpenSSL) and archive both the raw command output and a tiny status file for later manual review.

Carry out the following steps **using no more than four normal shell commands** (pipes and redirects are fine and count as part of the commands).  You do NOT need root privileges.

Required end-state of the system
1. A new directory must exist at  
   /home/user/vuln_scan  
   (create it if it is not already present).

2. Inside that directory there must be **two** files with the exact names below.  
   a. /home/user/vuln_scan/openssl_policy.out  
      • This file must contain the unaltered, complete stdout produced by running the command  
        apt-cache policy openssl  
        (no stderr, no extra lines). The file must be non-empty.  
   b. /home/user/vuln_scan/scan_report.log  
      • This is a three-line plain-text summary file that must be **exactly**:

        [OPENSSL QUICK SCAN]
        installed_package_info: openssl_policy.out
        scan_status: complete

      • No leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
      • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. the newline after the word “complete”).

3. File and directory permissions can follow the default umask (the grader only checks for existence and content).

4. Nothing else should be created or modified outside /home/user/vuln_scan.

Hints (not commands):  
• You will probably need to 1) create the directory, 2) run the apt-cache policy command, 3) redirect its output, and 4) echo or printf the three required lines into the log file.  
• All of that can easily be done with three or four commands total.

The automated tests will verify:  
• /home/user/vuln_scan exists and is a directory.  
• /home/user/vuln_scan/openssl_policy.out exists, is a regular file, and its size is > 0 bytes.  
• /home/user/vuln_scan/scan_report.log exists and its content matches the **exact** three-line block shown above (including the final newline).

Complete the task in the terminal and ensure no superfluous output is left on screen.
truth:
Expected post-task filesystem state:

Directory:
- /home/user/vuln_scan   (regular directory)

Files and required content/attributes:
1. /home/user/vuln_scan/openssl_policy.out  
   • must exist  
   • must contain at least one byte (grader uses “test -s”)  
   • content itself is NOT strictly checked

2. /home/user/vuln_scan/scan_report.log  
   Exact byte sequence (including final newline):
[OPENSSL QUICK SCAN]\n
installed_package_info: openssl_policy.out\n
scan_status: complete\n

(do not include literal “\n”; they represent ASCII newline 0x0A)

No other files should exist in /home/user/vuln_scan.
